ns0:entry.ns0:title: CoLM-SYSU/CoLM
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/CoLM-SYSU/CoLM
Language: fortran
The Common Land Model

ns0:entry.ns0:title: E3SM-Project/E3SM
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/E3SM-Project/E3SM
Language: fortran
Energy Exascale Earth System Model source code. NOTE: use "maint" branches for your work. Head of master is not validated.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: ESCOMP/CTSM
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/ESCOMP/CTSM
Language: fortran
Community Terrestrial Systems Model (includes the Community Land Model of CESM)

ns0:entry.ns0:title: ElmerCSC/elmerfem
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/ElmerCSC/elmerfem
Language: fortran
Official git repository of Elmer FEM software

ns0:entry.ns0:title: ExtremeFLOW/neko
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/ExtremeFLOW/neko
Language: fortran
/ᐠ. ｡.ᐟ\ᵐᵉᵒʷˎˊ˗

ns0:entry.ns0:title: GEOS-ESM/MAPL
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/GEOS-ESM/MAPL
Language: fortran
MAPL is a foundation layer of the GEOS architecture, whose original purpose is to supplement the Earth System Modeling Framework (ESMF)

ns0:entry.ns0:title: JuliaPackaging/Yggdrasil
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/JuliaPackaging/Yggdrasil
Language: fortran
Collection of builder repositories for BinaryBuilder.jl

ns0:entry.ns0:title: MODFLOW-ORG/modflow6
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/MODFLOW-ORG/modflow6
Language: fortran
USGS Modular Hydrologic Model

ns0:entry.ns0:title: MPAS-Dev/MPAS-Model
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/MPAS-Dev/MPAS-Model
Language: fortran
Repository for MPAS models and shared framework releases.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: NOAA-EMC/NCEPLIBS-bufr
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/NOAA-EMC/NCEPLIBS-bufr
Language: fortran
The NCEPLIBS-bufr library contains routines and utilites for working with the WMO BUFR format.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: NOAA-EMC/UPP
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/NOAA-EMC/UPP
Language: fortran

ns0:entry.ns0:title: NOAA-EMC/ufsatm
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/NOAA-EMC/ufsatm
Language: fortran

ns0:entry.ns0:title: NOAA-GFDL/GFDL_atmos_cubed_sphere
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/NOAA-GFDL/GFDL_atmos_cubed_sphere
Language: fortran
The GFDL atmos_cubed_sphere dynamical core code

ns0:entry.ns0:title: OpenFAST/openfast
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/OpenFAST/openfast
Language: fortran
Main repository for the NREL-supported OpenFAST whole-turbine and FAST.Farm wind farm simulation codes.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: ROCm/aomp
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/ROCm/aomp
Language: fortran
AOMP is an open source Clang/LLVM based compiler with added support for the OpenMP® API on Radeon™ GPUs. Use this repository for releases, issues, documentation, packaging, and examples.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: USEPA/CMAQ
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/USEPA/CMAQ
Language: fortran
Code for U.S. EPA’s Community Multiscale Air Quality Model (CMAQ) for estimating ozone, particulates, toxics, and deposition of acids and nutrients at neighborhood to global scales.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: danieljprice/phantom
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/danieljprice/phantom
Language: fortran
Phantom Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics and Magnetohydrodynamics code

ns0:entry.ns0:title: esmf-org/esmf
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/esmf-org/esmf
Language: fortran
The Earth System Modeling Framework (ESMF) is a suite of software tools for developing high-performance, multi-component Earth science modeling applications.

ns0:entry.ns0:title: firemodels/fds
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/firemodels/fds
Language: fortran
Fire Dynamics Simulator

ns0:entry.ns0:title: geoschem/geos-chem
ns0:entry.ns0:content:
URL: https://github.com/geoschem/geos-chem
Language: fortran
GEOS-Chem "Science Codebase" repository. Contains GEOS-Chem science routines, run directory generation scripts, and interface code. This repository is used as a submodule within the GCClassic and GCHP wrappers, as well as in other modeling contexts (external ESMs).
